description: Preset Speckle streams for E2E testing and demos - Enterprise Test Data Strategy

metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for demo BIM models used in testing and demonstrations
metadata.usage: Referenced by E2E tests, development env vars, and demo configurations
metadata.validation: All streams validated against live Speckle Server (v2.25.4)

streams.name: Demo Building - Duplex Architecture
streams.purpose: PRIMARY DEMO STREAM - Used for all E2E tests and developer demos

speckleUser.name: e
speckleUser.note: Test user account in Speckle Server

futureStreams.name: Demo Building - Simple Cube
futureStreams.description: 50KB basic geometry for quick smoke tests
futureStreams.estimatedEffort: 1 hour (create cube in Blender, export IFC, upload to Speckle)

futureStreams.name: Demo Building - Large Infrastructure
futureStreams.description: 50MB+ bridge with terrain and heavy geometry
futureStreams.purpose: Performance testing and memory management validation
futureStreams.estimatedEffort: 2 hours (find open-source bridge model, convert to IFC)

testDataStrategy.principle: One Primary Stream, Multiple Test Cases
testDataStrategy.rationale: Single high-quality demo model covers most E2E scenarios without data proliferation
expansionTriggers: Smoke tests need faster load times (<1s)
Specific IFC features need validation (e.g., MEP, structural)

setupInstructions.automated: Stream ea14da05a6 already exists in Speckle Server - no setup required
manual: 2. Create new stream with ID: ea14da05a6
4. Note the object ID from Speckle UI
5. Update this file with new object ID
validation: Expected: HTTP 200 with JSON object data
Alternative: Run E2E tests - tests/playwright/api-health-assessment.spec.ts validates Speckle connectivity

causes: Stream was deleted or ID changed
solutions: Check Speckle Server is running: docker ps | grep speckle-server
Verify stream exists in Speckle UI: http://localhost:8080
Get latest object ID from Speckle UI
Update this file and .env.development with new object ID

viewerNotRendering.symptoms: BIM viewer shows 'Upload an IFC file' message
causes: React app not rebuilt after env var changes
solutions: Rebuild web-dashboard: pnpm nx build web-dashboard
Restart dev server: docker compose restart web-dashboard
Check browser console for detailed error messages
